text: Code refactoring means improving the internal structure of your code without changing what it does. Think of it like reorganizing a messy room: you keep all the same items, but you arrange them more logically. The main goal is to make the code easier to read, understand, and maintain in the future. For example, you might rename unclear variable names or break a long function into smaller, simpler parts. This process helps reduce "technical debt," which is the extra work caused by writing messy or rushed code in the first place. Regular refactoring saves time later because clean code is much easier to update and fix.
